description: Reference Data types and values for VendorShield regulation tree configuration
workflow: STEP 1: Navigate to Settings → General → Reference Data
STEP 2: Click the 'Type' column header or filter to see all existing Reference Data types
STEP 3: For each type below, check if it already exists. If it does, review existing values and add only the missing ones. If it doesn't exist, create the new type first, then add all values.
STEP 4: When creating properties (execution_plan_regulations_tree.json, etc.), Reference Data properties point to these types by name.
STEP 5: When importing data, the import column contains the display value (e.g., 'Framework') — PU resolves it against the Reference Data type automatically.
notes: Reference Data types and values must exist BEFORE creating properties that reference them.
Some types (like 'Criticality') may already exist in the environment — always check first.
Values are case-sensitive in PU. Ensure consistent casing across import templates.
The 'Type' field in Reference Data is essentially the list/category name.
Each value under a type becomes a selectable option in any 'Reference Data – Select One' property that points to that type.

pre_check.description: Before adding anything, inventory existing Reference Data types in the environment.
steps: Navigate to Settings → General → Reference Data
For types that already exist below, compare values — only add net-new values
For types that don't exist, create the Type first, then add values
